definition: light, playful, teasing talk or banter
example: The friends filled the car ride with cheerful persiflage and jokes.

definition: the quality of holding firmly to a purpose or opinion; stubborn determination
example: Her pertinacity helped her finish the puzzle even when it seemed impossible.

definition: loving or living on plants, as certain insects do
example: Many phytophilous beetles spend their whole lives on a single leafy bush.

definition: small furry mammals related to rabbits that live among rocks in mountains
example: The hikers spotted several pikas darting between the boulders.

definition: a garden or plantation of pine trees and other conifers grown for study or display
example: We walked through the pinetum to see how many kinds of pine trees grow there.

definition: held or bound so as to prevent movement, especially by fastening the arms or wings
example: The gentle rescuers pinioned the injured bird's wing so it could heal.

definition: a sea mammal with flippers, such as a seal, sea lion, or walrus
example: A seal is a pinniped that uses its flippers to swim and to shuffle on land.

definition: feeding mainly on fish
example: The osprey is a piscivorous bird that dives into lakes to catch fish.

definition: a small rocky or icy body that can join with others to form a planet
example: Scientists think Earth grew as many a tiny planetesimal crashed together long ago.

definition: a deep, loud, and often mournful quality of sound
example: The plangency of the church bells echoed across the quiet valley.

definition: the name of a royal family that ruled England from the twelfth to the fifteenth century
example: King Richard the Lionheart belonged to the Plantagenet line of English kings.

definition: a diplomat given full power to act on behalf of their government
example: The queen sent a trusted plenipotentiary to sign the peace treaty.

definition: a complete coward
example: In the story, the knight refused to be a poltroon and faced the dragon bravely.

definition: the condition of being born with more than the usual number of fingers or toes
example: Some cats have polydactyly, giving them extra toes that look like mittens.

definition: a hairstyle in which the hair is swept up high above the forehead
example: The singer styled his hair into a tall pompadour before the show.

definition: a word made by blending the sounds and meanings of two other words; also a large travel suitcase
example: The word brunch is a portmanteau of breakfast and lunch.

definition: a strong liking or preference for something
example: She has a predilection for stories about clever, brave girls.

definition: having or showing knowledge of events before they happen
example: His prescient warning about the storm helped everyone reach safety in time.

definition: skillful quickness of the hands, especially in performing magic tricks; sleight of hand
example: With amazing prestidigitation, the magician made the coin vanish.

definition: a small round pastry filled with cream and often topped with chocolate
example: For dessert we each had a profiterole covered in warm chocolate sauce.

definition: reckless and wasteful spending or use of something
example: The king's profligacy left the kingdom's treasury nearly empty.

definition: to predict or foretell what will happen in the future
example: The old sailor could prognosticate the weather just by studying the clouds.

definition: relating to the body's sense of where its own parts are and how they move
example: Closing your eyes and touching your nose uses your proprioceptive sense.

definition: of or occurring in the next month
example: The letter said the meeting would be held on the tenth proximo.

definition: an itchy feeling of the skin
example: The rash caused a pruritus that made him want to scratch his arm.

definition: a drooping or sagging of a body part, especially the upper eyelid
example: The doctor noticed a slight ptosis in the patient's left eyelid.

definition: great physical beauty
example: The poem praised the pulchritude of the mountains at sunrise.

definition: showing a lack of courage; timid and fearful
example: It was pusillanimous of him to run away instead of helping his friend.

definition: a sudden attempt to overthrow a government by force
example: The history book described a failed putsch against the old ruler.

definition: the opening at the lower end of the stomach that leads into the small intestine
example: Food passes through the pylorus on its way from the stomach to the intestines.

definition: the fifteenth century, especially in relation to Italian art and culture
example: The museum's quattrocento paintings came from Italy in the 1400s.

definition: often complaining in a whining or fretful way
example: The tired toddler grew querulous and fussed about everything.

definition: a person who is eager to know the latest news and gossip; a busybody
example: The town quidnunc always seemed to know everyone's news before they did.

definition: a leafy vegetable with reddish-purple leaves and a slightly bitter taste, used in salads
example: She added a few crisp leaves of radicchio to brighten the salad.

definition: a garden flower with bright, many-petaled blooms, related to the buttercup
example: A pink ranunculus was the prettiest flower in the whole bouquet.

definition: the return of friendly relations between people or countries after a disagreement
example: The two neighbors reached a happy rapprochement after years of quarreling.

definition: a follower of a religious movement that began in Jamaica, honoring Emperor Haile Selassie
example: The rastafarian musician sang songs about peace and unity.

definition: a restaurant or tavern located in a basement
example: They ate hearty sausages in a cozy rathskeller beneath the old town hall.

definition: a tangy French sauce flavored with herbs, capers, and vinegar
example: The chef spooned a bright green ravigote over the steamed vegetables.

definition: a soft, orange-red mineral once used to make paint and dyes
example: The museum displayed a chunk of realgar that glowed a deep orange-red.

definition: politics based on practical goals and real conditions rather than ideals
example: The leader practiced realpolitik, making deals to keep his country safe.

definition: quick, clever, and witty conversation or replies
example: The two comedians delighted the crowd with their sharp repartee.

definition: a net or grid, such as a fine mesh used as a background in lace or on a map
example: The old lace had a delicate reseau of tiny knotted threads.

definition: a bugle or drum signal sounded early in the morning to wake soldiers
example: At dawn the reveille rang out and the whole camp woke up.

definition: an underground plant stem that grows sideways and sends up new shoots and roots
example: Ginger grows from a knobby rhizome that spreads under the soil.

definition: relating to or living on the banks of a river or stream
example: Willows and reeds grow in the riparian zone along the creek.

definition: the tendency or ability to laugh
example: The clown's silly antics tickled the risibility of everyone in the room.

definition: a small fried patty or roll of minced meat, often coated in breadcrumbs
example: For lunch we had a crispy rissole with mashed potatoes.

definition: in music, played with a sudden holding back or slowing of the tempo
example: The teacher marked the last few notes ritenuto so we would slow down.

definition: a short poem with a fixed pattern of lines and repeating opening words
example: For class she wrote a cheerful rondeau about springtime.

definition: a substance that makes the skin turn red, often used to soothe sore muscles
example: The warming rub was a rubefacient that made his aching shoulder feel better.

definition: having a wrinkled or ridged surface
example: The old oak leaf was dry and rugose, full of tiny wrinkles.

definition: living or growing among rocks
example: Certain rupicolous ferns cling to the cracks in the cliff face.

definition: relating to the quick, jerky movements the eyes make when shifting from one point to another
example: When you read a page, your eyes make fast saccadic jumps from word to word.

definition: relating to priests or the priesthood
example: The old book described the sacerdotal robes worn during the ceremony.

definition: resembling or having the qualities of soap; soapy
example: The clay felt smooth and saponaceous, almost slippery like soap.

definition: gloomy, serious, or slow to smile in manner
example: The saturnine guard rarely spoke and never seemed to grin.

definition: a leafy houseplant with glossy leaves arranged like the spokes of an umbrella
example: A tall schefflera stood in the corner, its shiny leaves spreading wide.

definition: a sparkle or flash of light; the act of twinkling
example: The scintillation of the stars made the night sky look alive.

definition: a person whose job long ago was to write out documents and letters by hand
example: In the old story, the scrivener copied the king's letters with a feather pen.

definition: making or spreading rude, insulting, or untrue remarks about someone
example: The newspaper apologized for printing scurrilous rumors about the mayor.

definition: showing great care, effort, and persistence in one's work
example: Her sedulous practice every day made her a wonderful violinist.

definition: a back-and-forth sloshing wave on the surface of an enclosed body of water like a lake
example: A strong wind caused a seiche that rocked the boats in the harbor.

definition: an official in a medieval household who managed the affairs of a lord or castle
example: The seneschal made sure the castle was ready for the king's visit.

definition: relating to a tomb or burial, or gloomy and dismal in a way that suggests one
example: The empty hallway had a cold, sepulchral silence.

definition: using or full of long words
example: His sesquipedalian speech was full of words nobody could understand.

definition: in music, played with sudden, strong emphasis on a note or chord
example: The drummer struck the cymbal sforzando to give the song a big accent.

definition: an image, copy, or imitation of something, especially one that is not quite real
example: The wax figure was a lifelike simulacrum of the famous inventor.

definition: the front and upper part of the skull or head
example: The doctor gently felt the baby's soft sinciput at the front of the head.

definition: a large plant-eating sea mammal, such as a manatee or dugong
example: A manatee is a gentle sirenian that grazes on underwater plants.

definition: a person who stays somewhere for a short time before moving on
example: The tired sojourner rested at the inn for one night before continuing the trip.

definition: a mistake in the use of grammar, or a breach of good manners
example: Saying "me and him went" is a common solecism that his teacher gently corrected.

definition: feeling or causing a strong desire to sleep; drowsy
example: The warm afternoon sun made the whole class feel somnolent.

definition: a tall grain-bearing grass grown for food, animal feed, and syrup
example: The farmer planted rows of sorghum to feed the cattle over winter.

definition: relating to or like a sister
example: The two friends shared a sororal bond, almost as close as real sisters.

definition: a type of black tea made from large leaves, often with a smoky flavor
example: Grandpa poured a cup of smoky souchong and told us a story.

definition: a mineral that is an important source of lithium
example: The geologist found a pale crystal of spodumene in the rocky hillside.

definition: inflammation of the bones of the spine
example: The doctor explained that spondylitis was making the man's back stiff and sore.

definition: a small pouch worn at the front of a Scottish kilt
example: He tucked his coins into the leather sporran hanging from his kilt.

definition: an organized group of aircraft, ships, or soldiers
example: A squadron of jets roared overhead during the airshow.

definition: an upright bar or post used as a support or barrier
example: A single metal stanchion held up the rope that guided the line.

definition: a person whose job is loading and unloading ships at a port
example: The stevedore carefully hauled the heavy crates onto the dock.

definition: the pair of foot supports that hang from a horse's saddle
example: She slipped her boots into the stirrups before the horse began to trot.

definition: a violin or other stringed instrument made by the Stradivari family, prized for its fine sound
example: The famous musician played a rare stradivarius at the concert.

definition: a very poisonous substance obtained from certain plant seeds
example: The mystery novel revealed that the villain had used strychnine as a poison.

definition: able to stop bleeding by drawing tissue together
example: The barber dabbed a styptic pencil on the tiny nick to stop the bleeding.

definition: fruit or fruit peel preserved in sugar; candied fruit
example: The fruitcake was dotted with bits of sweet orange succade.

definition: the act of shaking something vigorously, especially in medical examination
example: By gentle succussion of the bottle, she mixed the two liquids together.

definition: spread through or over with color, light, or feeling
example: The evening sky was suffused with soft shades of pink and gold.

definition: a groove or furrow, especially one on the surface of the brain
example: In biology class we learned that each sulcus in the brain lies between two folds.

definition: a correct word or expression that replaces a familiar but incorrect one
example: The editor insisted on the proper sumpsimus rather than the common mistake.

definition: behaving as though you think you are better than other people
example: The supercilious waiter looked down his nose at the ordinary customers.

definition: a stop or pause; relief from something
example: The travelers longed for a surcease from the long, tiring journey.

definition: an amount that is too much of something
example: After the holidays there was a surfeit of leftover cookies in the kitchen.

definition: a soft murmuring or whispering sound
example: A gentle susurrus of leaves filled the quiet forest.

definition: an imaginary graceful spirit of the air, or a slender graceful person
example: The dancer moved across the stage as lightly as a sylph.

definition: a brief loss of consciousness caused by too little blood reaching the brain; fainting
example: Standing up too fast in the heat can cause syncope and a sudden fall.

definition: a figure of speech in which a part stands for the whole or the whole for a part
example: Saying "all hands on deck" is a synecdoche, since "hands" means the whole sailors.

definition: a mixing of the senses, such as seeing colors when hearing sounds
example: Because of her synesthesia, she saw a bright blue color whenever a trumpet played.

definition: an official meeting of church leaders to discuss matters of religion
example: The bishops gathered at the synod to decide on new church rules.

definition: the straight-line alignment of three celestial bodies, such as the sun, earth, and moon
example: During a syzygy, the sun, earth, and moon line up to create an eclipse.

definition: a North American larch tree that sheds its needles in winter
example: The tamarack turned golden yellow before dropping its needles in autumn.

definition: a small brightly colored songbird of the Americas
example: A scarlet tanager flashed red among the green leaves.

definition: the prime minister, or head of government, of Ireland
example: The taoiseach gave a speech to the Irish parliament today.

definition: a large sheet of strong waterproof material used as a cover
example: We stretched a tarpaulin over the woodpile to keep the rain off.

definition: relating to a natural acid found in grapes and other fruits
example: The sour taste of grapes comes partly from tartaric acid.

definition: a person dressed in ragged, torn clothing
example: The friendly tatterdemalion in the play wore a coat full of patches and holes.

definition: relating to or resembling a bull
example: The strong, taurine shape of the statue made it look like a charging bull.

definition: a smooth slippery coating used on pans so food does not stick
example: The eggs slid right off the teflon pan without sticking.

definition: supposed knowledge of distant events gained without using the ordinary senses
example: The fortune teller claimed her telegnosis let her see things happening far away.

definition: an earthquake
example: A small temblor rattled the dishes but did no real damage.

definition: a shaped end of wood made to fit into a matching hole to join two pieces
example: The carpenter carved a tenon to slot neatly into the table leg.

definition: the Greek muse of dancing and choral song
example: In the myth, dancers prayed to Terpsichore for grace on the stage.

definition: relating to the ancient Germanic peoples or to Germany
example: The old castle had a stern, teutonic look with its heavy stone towers.

definition: a part of the brain that relays sensory signals to other brain areas
example: The thalamus helps pass along messages from your eyes and ears to the brain.

definition: relating to the sea, especially smaller or inland seas
example: The scientist studied thalassic creatures that live in the shallow sea.

definition: a person who performs miracles or magic; a worker of wonders
example: In the tale, a wise thaumaturge turned stones into loaves of bread.

definition: a protective case or covering around a plant or animal part
example: The tiny seed was tucked safely inside its hard theca.

definition: an attempt to explain why a good and powerful God allows evil and suffering
example: The philosophy class discussed theodicy and why bad things happen to good people.

definition: helping to heal or make someone feel better
example: A quiet walk in the woods can be very therapeutic after a busy week.

definition: a song or poem of mourning for someone who has died
example: The choir sang a gentle threnody in memory of the old king.

definition: an extinct meat-eating marsupial of Australia, also called the Tasmanian tiger
example: The museum showed a photograph of the last known thylacine.

definition: a light midday meal or lunch, especially in India
example: She packed rice and curry in a metal box for her tiffin.

definition: a bright golden or reddish-brown color, especially of hair
example: Her long titian hair glowed like copper in the sunlight.

definition: a lively piece of music, often for keyboard, showing off the player's skill
example: The organist filled the hall with a fast, thundering toccata.

definition: the soft green digestive part found inside a lobster, eaten by some as a delicacy
example: Some seafood lovers scoop out the tomalley from the lobster and spread it on bread.

definition: a way of making pictures of the inside of the body in thin slices, as with a CT scan
example: The hospital used tomography to take detailed images of the patient's chest.

definition: a tall white hat worn by a chef, or a close-fitting brimless hat
example: The chef adjusted his tall white toque before entering the kitchen.

definition: a bullfighter, especially one who fights on horseback
example: The toreador waved a bright red cape in the bullring.

definition: shaped like a ring or doughnut
example: A doughnut and an inner tube both have a toroidal shape.

definition: the ability of a single cell to develop into any type of cell or a whole organism
example: The totipotency of a plant cell can let it grow into an entire new plant.

definition: a small round cut of beef from the tenderloin
example: The restaurant served a juicy tournedos with roasted vegetables.

definition: a North American songbird related to the sparrow, often seen scratching on the ground
example: A spotted towhee hopped through the bushes searching for seeds.

definition: handed down from one generation to another; traditional
example: The tralatitious folk tales had been passed along by grandparents for centuries.

definition: the quality of lasting only a short time
example: The transience of a rainbow makes it feel all the more magical.

definition: situated on or coming from the far side of the mountains
example: The travelers reached the transmontane valley hidden beyond the peaks.

definition: an illness caused by eating undercooked meat containing tiny parasitic worms
example: Cooking pork thoroughly helps prevent trichinosis.

definition: a condition in which a person feels a strong urge to pull out their own hair
example: The counselor helped the boy manage his trichotillomania and stop pulling his hair.

definition: relating to the large nerve that carries feeling from the face to the brain
example: The dentist explained that the trigeminal nerve gives feeling to the teeth and face.

definition: an amino acid found in food that the body needs to build proteins
example: Turkey contains tryptophan, which some people say makes them sleepy.

definition: a lizard-like reptile of New Zealand that is the last of an ancient group
example: The tuatara can live for more than a hundred years on its rocky island home.

definition: an ancient mound of earth raised over a grave; a burial mound
example: Archaeologists dug carefully into the grassy tumulus to study the old tomb.

definition: the empty space in a container that is not filled with liquid
example: The bottle was left with a small ullage so the juice would not overflow.

definition: to make a long, loud, wailing cry
example: In the distance we could hear the wolves ululate at the full moon.

definition: having nails or claws
example: Cats are unguiculate animals, with sharp claws on each paw.

definition: not usual or expected; out of the ordinary
example: The shy boy spoke up with unwonted boldness during the debate.

definition: often ill or overly worried about one's health
example: The valetudinary old gentleman kept a shelf full of medicines and tonics.

definition: fresh green plants or the lush green color of growing vegetation
example: After the spring rains, the hills were covered in bright verdure.

definition: truthful and matching what is real
example: The witness gave a veridical account of exactly what she had seen.

definition: the quality of seeming real or true to life
example: The film's careful costumes gave it great verisimilitude, as if we had traveled back in time.

definition: a wild plant with small blue or purple flowers, once used in herbal remedies
example: Grandmother grew vervain in her garden to make a soothing herbal tea.

definition: relating to or based on the number twenty
example: The old counting song used a vicenary system, grouping things in sets of twenty.

definition: to treat or speak of someone or something as having little worth
example: It is unkind to vilipend a friend's hard work just because it is different from your own.

definition: a small American songbird with mostly green or gray feathers
example: A tiny vireo sang from the treetop while we ate our picnic lunch.

definition: to spoil or weaken the quality of something
example: One careless mistake can vitiate an otherwise perfect science project.

definition: a warm spiced drink shared during holiday celebrations, especially at Christmas
example: The carolers sipped hot wassail to keep warm as they sang.

definition: an overflow channel that carries off extra water from a reservoir or canal
example: After the heavy rain, the extra water rushed out through the wasteweir.

definition: a feeling of sadness about the troubles of the world
example: Reading the sad news filled her with a quiet weltschmerz.

definition: a nocturnal North American bird named for its repeated call
example: At dusk we heard a whippoorwill calling its own name over and over.

definition: a small dome-shaped hut made of a frame covered with brush, bark, or mats, used by some Native American peoples
example: The museum showed how a wickiup was built from bent branches and woven grass.

definition: a plant whose leaves make a blue dye, or the dye itself
example: Long ago, people used woad to color their cloth a deep blue.

definition: a swordfish, a large ocean fish with a long sword-shaped upper jaw
example: The diver spotted a sleek xiphias slicing through the deep water.

definition: relating to or in the style of the Irish poet W. B. Yeats
example: Her dreamy poem about swans had a yeatsian feel to it.

definition: an Italian dessert made of whipped egg yolks, sugar, and sweet wine
example: For dessert the chef whisked up a light, frothy zabaglione.

definition: the study or practice of fermentation, such as in brewing or baking
example: He read a book on zymurgy to learn how yeast makes bread rise.
